description_long: This rifle features a robust, industrial design with a prominent multi-barrel rotary system, reminiscent of a Gatling or minigun style weapon. The main body is blocky and angular, with visible mechanical components and a large cylindrical housing at the front, likely for the rotating barrels. There is a raised mounting bracket or sight rail on top, suggesting compatibility with various optics or accessories. The rear section appears compact, lacking a traditional rifle stock, and the grip and trigger assembly are integrated into the main chassis. The overall finish is matte black, giving it a tactical and utilitarian appearance. The construction suggests heavy use of metal, with some possible polymer or composite elements for secondary components.
description: A heavy, multi-barrel rotary rifle with a matte black finish, blocky body, and prominent cylindrical barrel assembly. Features a top rail and compact rear section, evoking a minigun-style design.
description_view_0: Front view shows the circular arrangement of the barrels and the central axis of the rotary system.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the length of the barrels, the blocky main body, and the top mounting rail.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the compact back end and the alignment of the barrels, with minimal stock structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the mechanical details of the body and the full length of the barrel assembly.
